metrics.name: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
metrics.definition: Sum of monthly recurring revenue from all active subscriptions

metrics.name: Churn Rate
metrics.definition: Percentage of subscriptions cancelled in a period relative to total subscriptions
metrics.calculation: COUNT(cancelled) / COUNT(total) per month, grouped by plan

metrics.name: Net Revenue Retention (NRR)
metrics.definition: Revenue retained from existing customers including expansion and contraction

metrics.name: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
metrics.definition: Average revenue per customer over their entire lifecycle
metrics.calculation: SUM(invoices.amount WHERE status='paid') grouped by customer

metrics.name: Average Revenue Per Account (ARPA)
metrics.definition: Average MRR across all active subscriptions

metrics.name: Customer Acquisition Cost Efficiency
metrics.definition: Revenue contribution segmented by acquisition source

metrics.name: Support Satisfaction (CSAT)
metrics.definition: Average customer satisfaction score from resolved support tickets
metrics.calculation: AVG(satisfaction_score) WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL AND satisfaction_score IS NOT NULL

metrics.name: Usage Health Score
metrics.definition: Composite score based on API calls, active users, and storage relative to plan capacity
metrics.calculation: Weighted average of normalized usage metrics per customer

business_rules: MRR should only count subscriptions with status = 'active' (not 'cancelled', 'upgraded', or 'downgraded')
Annual billing customers get a 10% discount — their invoice amount is mrr * 12 * 0.9
A customer can have multiple subscription records — upgrades/downgrades close the old and create a new one
Churn rate should be calculated monthly for meaningful comparison across plans
Net Revenue Retention above 100% means expansion revenue exceeds lost revenue
Usage metrics are sampled (3-5 data points per month per customer) — use averages for analysis

common_gotchas.solution: Use status = 'active' OR ended_at IS NULL — both work. Do NOT count 'upgraded'/'downgraded' as active since new subscription row exists.
ended_at IS NULL means the subscription is still active. Use COALESCE or IS NULL checks in date range filters.
About 30% of resolved tickets have no rating. Use AVG(satisfaction_score) which ignores NULLs, but note the sample bias.
When calculating current MRR per customer, use the subscription with status = 'active'. For historical analysis, use started_at/ended_at ranges.

common_gotchas.issue: Usage data is sampled, not daily
common_gotchas.solution: 3-5 data points per customer per month. Use AVG() for per-day estimates or SUM() for monthly totals, but note they're approximate.
